question.text: A trader buys a call at strike Rs. 100 (premium Rs. 3) and simultaneously sells a call at strike Rs. 105 (premium Rs. 1). What is the maximum profit of this bull call spread?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Net premium paid = 3 - 1 = Rs. 2. Max profit = (Higher Strike - Lower Strike) - Net Premium = (105 - 100) - 2 = Rs. 3. **The Trap:** Rs. 5 is the strike difference without deducting net premium. Rs. 2 is the net premium (which is actually the max loss, not max profit). **Cross-Exam Context:** Spread strategies are tested in NISM Equity Derivatives with the same payoff logic. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In a bull call spread, max profit is capped (unlike a naked long call). The trade-off is a lower cost of entry (net premium paid).
